description: Phase 6 — AI & Webhook Workers: call_ai worker (auto_send: false stores draft in execution_step.output; auto_send: true performs synthetic Messaging Service send with auto_send_respects_active_hours flag); call_webhook worker (secret resolver injection for {{key}} header values, AbortController timeout → immediate step failure with no BullMQ retry); AWS Secrets Manager resolver + wiring of both workers into index.ts.

userStories.title: call_ai worker — AI Service call, store draft in output, auto_send paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a call_ai action worker that calls the AI Service to generate a draft, stores it in execution_step.output, and either completes the step (auto_send: false) or performs a synthetic Messaging Service send (auto_send: true) with optional active hours gating controlled by the auto_send_respects_active_hours flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-workers/call-ai.worker.ts`

Import `type { Job, Queue } from 'bullmq'`; import `type { ActionJobData } from '../../queue/index.js'`; import `type { ExecutionRepository } from '../../repositories/execution.repository.js'`; import `{ resolveParams, type ExecutionContext } from '../field-interpolator.js'`; import `{ computeDelay, type ActiveHoursConfig } from '../active-hours.js'`
Export `const AI_SERVICE_URL_ENV = 'AI_SERVICE_URL'`
Export `const MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL_ENV = 'MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL'` (independent constant — same value as in send-message.worker.ts, but exported from this file)
Export `createCallAiProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>, fetchFn: typeof fetch = globalThis.fetch): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`

Processor step 1: destructure `{ execution_id, step_id, action_params, exec_ctx, event, active_hours }` from `job.data`. Mark step running: `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`

Processor step 2 (resolve params): `const resolved = resolveParams(action_params, event, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`. Extract: `const nextStepId = resolved['_next_step_id'] as string | undefined`; `const promptId = resolved['prompt_id'] as string`; `const context = resolved['context']`; `const model = resolved['model'] as string | undefined`; `const dedupKey = resolved['dedup_key'] as string | undefined`; `const autoSend = resolved['auto_send'] === true`; `const autoSendRespectsActiveHours = resolved['auto_send_respects_active_hours'] === true`; `const to = resolved['to_field'] as string | undefined`; `const from = resolved['from_field'] as string | undefined`

Processor step 3 (call AI Service): `const aiUrl = process.env[AI_SERVICE_URL_ENV]`. If !aiUrl: throw `new Error('AI_SERVICE_URL is not configured')`. Build payload: `const aiPayload: Record<string, unknown> = { prompt_id: promptId, context }`. If `model` is defined: `aiPayload['model'] = model`. If `dedupKey` is defined: `aiPayload['dedup_key'] = dedupKey`. Call `const aiRes = await fetchFn(aiUrl + '/ai/complete', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify(aiPayload) })`. If `!aiRes.ok`: throw `new Error('ai/complete error: ' + aiRes.status)`. Parse: `const { ai_draft } = (await aiRes.json()) as { ai_draft: string }`

Processor step 4a — auto_send: false path: if `!autoSend`: `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date(), output: { ai_draft } })`. If `nextStepId`: `const nextStep = await repo.findStepById(nextStepId)`. If non-null: `await queue.add(nextStep.action_type, { execution_id, step_id: nextStepId, action_type: nextStep.action_type, action_params: nextStep.action_params as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx, event, active_hours: job.data.active_hours })`. Else: `await repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', new Date())`. Then `return`

Processor step 4b — auto_send: true path: if `autoSendRespectsActiveHours && active_hours != null && active_hours !== undefined`: `const delay = computeDelay(active_hours as ActiveHoursConfig, event as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`. If `delay > 0`: `await job.moveToDelayed(Date.now() + delay, job.token); return` (step stays 'running'; AI called again on retry via dedup_key, giving same result)

Processor step 4c — auto_send: true, send phase: `const msgUrl = process.env[MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL_ENV]`. If !msgUrl: throw `new Error('MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL is not configured')`. `const autoSendDedupKey = (exec_ctx as ExecutionContext).event_id + '-ai-autosend'`. `const msgPayload: Record<string, unknown> = { to, from, body: ai_draft, dedup_key: autoSendDedupKey }`. `const msgRes = await fetchFn(msgUrl + '/messages/send', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify(msgPayload) })`. If `!msgRes.ok`: throw `new Error('messages/send error: ' + msgRes.status)`. `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date(), output: { ai_draft } })`. Then chain next step or complete execution (same pattern as auto_send: false)

Create `test/unit/action-workers/call-ai-worker.test.ts`; mock `computeDelay` via `vi.mock('../../../src/services/active-hours.js', () => ({ computeDelay: vi.fn() }))`; mock `repo` and `queue` with `vi.fn()`; inject `fetchFn: vi.fn()`; add `moveToDelayed: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)` to mock job object

Test: auto_send: false, with next step — `fetchFn` called once (AI only), step marked completed with `output: { ai_draft: 'draft text' }`, `repo.findStepById` called, `queue.add` called; `updateExecutionStatus` NOT called; Messaging NOT called
Test: auto_send: false, no next step — `fetchFn` called once, step completed with `output: { ai_draft }`, `updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', ...)` called; `queue.add` NOT called

Test: auto_send: true, autoSendRespectsActiveHours: false — `computeDelay` NOT called; `fetchFn` called twice (AI + Messaging); Messaging call body includes `{ to, from, body: ai_draft, dedup_key: '<event_id>-ai-autosend' }`; step completed with `output: { ai_draft }`

Test: auto_send: true, autoSendRespectsActiveHours: true, active_hours non-null, delay > 0 — `computeDelay` called; `job.moveToDelayed` called with `(Date.now() + delay, job.token)`; `fetchFn` called ONCE (AI only); Messaging NOT called; step NOT completed
Test: auto_send: true, autoSendRespectsActiveHours: true, delay = 0 — proceeds to Messaging send; step completed with output
Test: auto_send: true, autoSendRespectsActiveHours: true, active_hours: null — `computeDelay` NOT called; proceeds to Messaging send immediately
Test: AI_SERVICE_URL not set → throws containing 'AI_SERVICE_URL'; step NOT completed
Test: AI Service non-ok response → throws containing status code; step NOT completed; Messaging NOT called
Test: auto_send: true, MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL not set → AI called successfully; then throws containing 'MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL'; step NOT completed
Test: auto_send: true, Messaging non-ok → throws containing status code; step NOT completed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The active hours check for auto_send happens AFTER the AI call (unlike send_message/send_email where it precedes marking 'running'). This is intentional: the AI call itself always executes immediately; only the synthetic send respects active hours. On retry after moveToDelayed, step is marked 'running' again (attempt incremented) and AI called again — this is fine because the dedup_key prevents duplicate AI side effects. The `output: { ai_draft }` extras field in `updateStepStatus` is already supported by the existing ExecutionRepository (column `output` is serialized as JSON). Do NOT modify the repository. For the AI request payload, only include `model` if defined (undefined model → field omitted, AI Service uses its configured default). The `store_as` field from action_params is NOT forwarded to AI Service — the worker always stores the result under the key `ai_draft` regardless. For the mock job: use `exec_ctx: { event_id: 'evt-1', execution_id: 'exec-1', rule_id: 'rule-1', rule_version: 1 }` so that `(exec_ctx as ExecutionContext).event_id + '-ai-autosend'` evaluates to `'evt-1-ai-autosend'` in assertions. Use `vi.fn().mockResolvedValueOnce(...)` chain for multiple fetch calls.

userStories.title: call_webhook worker — secret resolution in headers + timeout → immediate step failure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a call_webhook action worker that resolves {{key}} template patterns in request headers from an injected secretsResolver function, calls an external HTTP URL, and handles timeout by immediately failing the step (no BullMQ retry) while allowing other errors to rethrow for normal backoff retry.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-workers/call-webhook.worker.ts`

Import `type { Job, Queue } from 'bullmq'`; import `type { ActionJobData } from '../../queue/index.js'`; import `type { ExecutionRepository } from '../../repositories/execution.repository.js'`; import `{ resolveParams, type ExecutionContext } from '../field-interpolator.js'`

Export `async function resolveHeaderSecrets(headers: Record<string, string>, secretsResolver: (key: string) => Promise<string>): Promise<Record<string, string>>` — iterates header entries; for each value matching `/^\{\{(\w+)\}\}$/`, calls `secretsResolver(match[1])` and replaces the value; non-matching values pass through unchanged

Export `function createCallWebhookProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>, secretsResolver: (key: string) => Promise<string>, fetchFn: typeof fetch = globalThis.fetch): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`
Processor step 1: mark step running: `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`

Processor step 2 (resolve params): `const resolved = resolveParams(action_params, event, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`. Extract: `const nextStepId = resolved['_next_step_id'] as string | undefined`; `const url = resolved['url'] as string`; `const method = (resolved['method'] as string | undefined) ?? 'POST'`; `const rawHeaders = (resolved['headers'] as Record<string, string> | undefined) ?? {}`; `const body = resolved['body']`; `const timeoutMs = (resolved['timeout_ms'] as number | undefined) ?? 5000`
Processor step 3 (resolve header secrets): `const resolvedHeaders = await resolveHeaderSecrets(rawHeaders, secretsResolver)`

Processor step 4 (HTTP call with timeout): create `const controller = new AbortController()` and `const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs)`. Build request body: `const requestBody = typeof body === 'string' ? body : JSON.stringify(body)`. Call: `let webhookRes: Response`. Wrap in try/catch: `webhookRes = await fetchFn(url, { method, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...resolvedHeaders }, body: requestBody, signal: controller.signal })`. In the catch block: `clearTimeout(timer)`. If `err instanceof Error && err.name === 'AbortError'`: `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'failed', { error: 'timeout after ' + timeoutMs + 'ms' })`; `await repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'failed', new Date())`; `return` — do NOT rethrow. Otherwise: rethrow `err`
After fetchFn call: `clearTimeout(timer)`. If `!webhookRes.ok`: throw `new Error('call_webhook error: ' + webhookRes.status)`

Processor step 5 (complete and chain): `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date() })`. If `nextStepId`: load next step and enqueue (same pattern as other workers, forward `active_hours: job.data.active_hours`). Else: `await repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', new Date())`
Create `test/unit/action-workers/call-webhook-worker.test.ts`; mock `secretsResolver: vi.fn()`; mock `fetchFn: vi.fn()`; mock `repo` and `queue` with `vi.fn()`

Test `resolveHeaderSecrets` directly: header with `{{my_secret}}` → `secretsResolver('my_secret')` called; returned value used; plain header value unchanged; mixed headers → only secret-pattern values resolved

Test: happy path, no secrets in headers, no next step — `fetchFn` called with correct URL, method, merged headers (Content-Type + resolvedHeaders), stringified body; step completed; `updateExecutionStatus` called; `queue.add` NOT called
Test: happy path, with next step — step completed; `queue.add` called with correct data including `active_hours: job.data.active_hours`; `updateExecutionStatus` NOT called

Test: timeout (AbortError) — `fetchFn` rejects with an error where `.name === 'AbortError'`; step set to `'failed'` with `error` containing `'timeout'`; `updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'failed', ...)` called; NO throw; `queue.add` NOT called; step NOT completed
Test: non-ok HTTP response (e.g. 500) — throws error containing '500'; step NOT completed; no updateExecutionStatus call (BullMQ retries)
Test: non-timeout network error (e.g. `new Error('ECONNREFUSED')` with name `'Error'`) — error is rethrown; step NOT completed
Test: default method is 'POST' when method not in action_params — `fetchFn` called with `method: 'POST'`
Test: default timeoutMs is 5000 when timeout_ms not in action_params — AbortController aborts after 5000ms (assert via mocking setTimeout or verifying the controller is configured correctly)
Test: body is object → JSON.stringify applied before passing to fetchFn; body is string → passed as-is
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The secret pattern `^{{(\w+)}}$` matches the ENTIRE header value — partial patterns like `Bearer {{token}}` are NOT resolved (leave that as a future enhancement; the spec only shows full-value secret patterns). Note: `resolveParams` runs BEFORE secret resolution — it processes `{{key}}` values via its TEMPLATE_TOKEN_RE which tries exec_ctx first, returning `{{key}}` unchanged if exec_ctx has no such field. The second-pass `resolveHeaderSecrets` then catches these unchanged `{{key}}` values and resolves them via AWS. For AbortError testing: `const err = new Error('aborted'); err.name = 'AbortError'; fetchFn.mockRejectedValue(err)`. Do NOT use `AbortSignal.timeout()` — use `AbortController` + `setTimeout` for compatibility with Node.js 24 and test mocking. For the `webhookRes` variable: declare `let webhookRes!: Response` outside the try/catch (TypeScript needs it initialized before use in the ok-check). The `resolveHeaderSecrets` helper is exported so it can be unit-tested directly — write the direct unit tests before the processor integration tests in the same test file.

userStories.title: AWS Secrets Manager resolver + wire Phase 6 workers into index.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an AWS Secrets Manager resolver function that satisfies the call_webhook secretsResolver contract, and both call_ai and call_webhook workers registered in the Fastify application alongside all existing Phase 5 workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `"@aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager": "^3.0.0"` to the `dependencies` section of `apps/platform/automation/package.json`
Create `src/services/secrets-resolver.ts`
Import `{ SecretsManagerClient, GetSecretValueCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager'`

Export `function createSecretsResolver(): (key: string) => Promise<string>` — creates a `new SecretsManagerClient({})` in closure scope, returns an async function that calls `client.send(new GetSecretValueCommand({ SecretId: key }))`, checks `response.SecretString` is truthy (throw `new Error('Secret not found or has no string value: ' + key)` if not), and returns `response.SecretString`
Modify `src/index.ts`: add `import { createCallAiProcessor } from './services/action-workers/call-ai.worker.js'`
Modify `src/index.ts`: add `import { createCallWebhookProcessor } from './services/action-workers/call-webhook.worker.js'`
Modify `src/index.ts`: add `import { createSecretsResolver } from './services/secrets-resolver.js'`
In `src/index.ts`, after `const jobCanceller = new JobCanceller(queue)` (before `fastify.register(rulesRoutes, ...)`), add `const secretsResolver = createSecretsResolver()`
In `src/index.ts`, add to the `workers` array: `createActionWorker(QUEUE_NAME, connection, createCallAiProcessor(execRepo, queue), fastify.log as Pick<Console, 'error'>)`
In `src/index.ts`, add to the `workers` array: `createActionWorker(QUEUE_NAME, connection, createCallWebhookProcessor(execRepo, queue, secretsResolver), fastify.log as Pick<Console, 'error'>)`
No new test file needed for this story — AWS Secrets Manager is injected as a function and already covered by call_webhook unit tests in US-002
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The `createSecretsResolver` factory creates ONE `SecretsManagerClient` instance and reuses it across all calls — do not create a new client per call. `SecretsManagerClient({})` uses default credential chain (IAM role in ECS). The `secretsResolver` is created alongside `jobCanceller` (after `const queue = createQueue(connection)`) and before `fastify.register(rulesRoutes, ...)` — this is where the current index.ts already places queue/jobCanceller. Add `secretsResolver` on the very next line after jobCanceller for locality. The workers for call_ai and call_webhook use `execRepo` (not `repo` — `repo` is the RulesRepository; `execRepo` is ExecutionRepository) — follow the exact same pattern as send_message and send_email workers in the array. After adding the AWS SDK package, run `npm install` or equivalent to update the lockfile before typechecking.
